population: Adults with possible gallstone symptoms; pregnancy, children, jaundice, pancreatitis, cholangitis, severe illness, and postoperative states require separate pathways.
intended_setting: Primary, gastroenterology, hepatology, colorectal, result-review, or outpatient follow-up as qualified by the exact source.

evidence_statements.faithful_clinical_statement: People have the right to be involved in discussions and make informed decisions about their care, as described in making decisions about your care . Making decisions using NICE guidelines explains how we use words to show the strength (or certainty) of our recommendations, and has information about prescribing medicines (including off-label use), professional guidelines, standards and laws (including on consent and mental capacity), and safeguarding.

evidence_statements.faithful_clinical_statement: Consider magnetic resonance cholangiopancreatography (MRCP) if ultrasound has not detected common bile duct stones but the: bile duct is dilated and/or liver function test results are abnormal.

evidence_statements.faithful_clinical_statement: Clear the bile duct: surgically at the time of laparoscopic cholecystectomy or with endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) before or at the time of laparoscopic cholecystectomy.

evidence_statements.faithful_clinical_statement: If the bile duct cannot be cleared with ERCP, use biliary stenting to achieve biliary drainage only as a temporary measure until definitive endoscopic or surgical clearance.
